---
title: "Minku Kang"
subtitle: "강민구 · Preventive medicine physician · Epidemiologist · Health policy researcher"
pagetitle: "Minku Kang"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - text: "Email (academic)"
      icon: envelope
      href: "mailto:minku93@korea.ac.kr"
    - text: "Email (general)"
      icon: envelope-paper
      href: "mailto:kangminku.kr@gmail.com"
    - text: "Google Scholar"
      icon: mortarboard
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=BUn--4YAAAAJ"
    - text: "ORCID"
      icon: person-badge
      href: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7226-8159"
    - text: "ResearchGate"
      icon: journal-text
      href: "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Minku-Kang-2"
    - text: "LinkedIn"
      icon: linkedin
      href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/kangminku/"
toc: false
---

## Welcome

I am a preventive medicine physician dreaming of finding practical solutions to
real-world inequalities through social epidemiology and public policy, ultimately
building a cohesive and inclusive society where diverse individuals can thrive
together in harmony. I received my M.D. and a B.A. in Sociology (with a minor in
Economics) from Korea University, and I am currently a Ph.D. candidate in Public
Health while pursuing a Master of Public Policy at the KDI School. I am a
board-certified preventive medicine physician, currently serving as an
Epidemiologist and Healthcare Management Officer at the Armed Forces Medical
Command in the Republic of Korea.

Through my roles as a Board Member of the Korean Society for Preventive Medicine
and Vice President of the Korean Association of Preventive Medicine Physicians, as
well as my past tenure as President of the Korean Intern Resident Association —
where I contributed to reforming resident working-hour regulations and received a
commendation from the Minister of Health and Welfare — I have continuously engaged
in healthcare policy and institutional reform.

[Download CV (PDF)](assets/CV_Minku_Kang_2026.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm target="_blank"}

## Biography

I graduated from Korea University with an M.D. and a B.A. in Sociology, with a minor
in Economics. During my undergraduate years, I served as the Vice President of the
Korea University Student Union, where I actively engaged in university and community
governance, focusing on youth housing and education policy issues. Following my
graduation, I completed my medical internship and residency in Preventive Medicine
at the Korea University Medical Center. Concurrently, I embarked on a Ph.D. program
in Public Health at Korea University, complemented by a Master of Public Policy (MPP)
program at the KDI School to build a strong analytical foundation for policy design.

During my residency, I became deeply involved in health policy and healthcare
workforce advocacy. I served as the President of the Korean Intern Resident
Association (KIRA), where I led efforts to improve working conditions and mental
health for young doctors. I acted as the Principal Investigator for the national
study on reforming the 36-hour consecutive duty system, and my efforts contributed
to crucial reforms in resident working-hour regulations. For these contributions, I
received a commendation from the Minister of Health and Welfare. I also represented
Korean junior doctors internationally as a delegate for the World Medical
Association – Junior Doctors Network (WMA-JDN).

After obtaining my board certification in Preventive Medicine, I began my military
service. I am currently serving as an Epidemiologist and Healthcare Management
Officer at the Armed Forces Medical Command (AFMC). In this capacity, I manage
public mental health and infectious disease prevention initiatives and lead research
projects assessing the mental health and healthcare utilization of military
personnel. Previously, I contributed to public health crisis management at the
COVID-19 Community Treatment Center in Seoul and served as a visiting researcher at
the National Center for Mental Health.

My primary research lies at the intersection of social epidemiology and social
policy. I develop methodological and applied research to understand how social
inequalities — such as precarious employment, long working hours, and area
deprivation — translate into adverse health and mental health outcomes. My recent
publications have addressed gender inequalities in mental health during the COVID-19
pandemic and the impact of national health screening programs. I am currently
completing my doctoral dissertation, which extensively analyzes working conditions,
sleep deprivation, and depressive symptoms among Korean medical residents.

Beyond research, I am deeply committed to community medicine and institutional
reform. I currently serve as a Board Member and the Chairperson of the Future
Generations Committee for the Korean Society for Preventive Medicine (KSPM), as well
as the Vice President and Founding Member of the Korean Association of Preventive
Medicine Physicians (KAPMP). Through these roles, and my participation in multiple
Ministry of Health and Welfare advisory committees, I continue to strive for
practical, preventive approaches to resolve structural issues and build a cohesive
society where diverse individuals can thrive together.

## Areas of interest

**Social determinants of health** — workplace mental health, suicide prevention,
social epidemiology, labour market, social policy, health inequalities, urban and
neighbourhood environment

**Preventive medicine** — clinical preventive medicine, health risk assessment,
health screening and management, population medicine, occupational medicine,
lifestyle medicine

**Health policy & management** — healthcare workers, health systems, healthcare
reform

## Current position

**Epidemiologist & Officer (Health Policy and Management)**<br>
Armed Forces Medical Command, Ministry of National Defense<br>
May 2025 – Apr 2027
